FORD MOTOR COMPANY
Material Specifications — Body Structure

2022 FORD F-150

MATERIAL CLASSIFICATION — BODY PANELS

ALUMINUM ALLOYS:
The 2022 F-150 body structure (cab, bed, doors, hood, fenders) uses:
- 5000-series aluminum alloy (cab structure, inner panels)
- 6000-series aluminum alloy (outer panels, bed sides)

Tensile strength:
- 5052-H32: 228 MPa yield / 262 MPa ultimate
- 6111-T4: 170 MPa yield / 290 MPa ultimate (outer panels)

HIGH STRENGTH STEEL (HSS):
Frame rails and cab mounts remain high strength steel:
- Grade: HSLA 550 (550 MPa yield strength)
- Frame rails: 3.0-4.5mm gauge
- Do not straighten — replace damaged frame sections

ULTRA HIGH STRENGTH STEEL (UHSS):
B-pillar inner reinforcement: 1500 MPa press-hardened steel
- Hot press formed: cannot be straightened, heat, or welded conventionally
- Replacement only — no repair permitted on 1500 MPa components

MATERIAL IDENTIFICATION:
Refer to Ford Body Parts Catalog for material identification by part number.
All aluminum panels are marked with recycling symbol and alloy code on inner face.

REPAIR RESTRICTIONS BY MATERIAL:
Material | Straighten | Heat | Spot Weld | MIG | Adhesive
Al 6000  |    No*     |  No  |  Alum eq  |  Al | Yes
Al 5000  |    Yes     |  No  |  Alum eq  |  Al | Yes
HSLA 550 |    Yes     | <649C|    Yes    | Yes | No
UHSS 1500|    No      |  No  |    No     |  No | No

*Minor straightening of 6000-series aluminum possible with specialized tools only
All aluminum work requires aluminum-certified equipment and technicians.

Tensile measurement units: MPa (megapascals); 1 ksi = 6.895 MPa
